text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form

text: The focus of parabola \( y^2 = 4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2 = 4ax \): vertex at origin, focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \(x=-a\).
name: Focus of Parabola

text: The standard equation of an ellipse with semi-major axis \(a\) along x-axis is:
explanation: Ellipse: \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) with \(a>b>0\). Foci at \( (\pm c,0) \) where \( c^2=a^2-b^2 \).
name: Ellipse Standard Form

explanation: Eccentricity of ellipse: \( e = \frac{c}{a} \) where \( c = \sqrt{a^2-b^2} \). Always \( 0 < e < 1 \).
name: Eccentricity of Ellipse

text: The standard equation of a hyperbola with transverse axis along x-axis is:
name: Hyperbola Standard Form

text: The eccentricity of a parabola is:
name: Parabola Eccentricity

explanation: Asymptotes of hyperbola: \( y = \pm\frac{b}{a}x \) (pass through center).
name: Asymptotes of Hyperbola

text: The latus rectum of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) has length:
explanation: Length of latus rectum of \( y^2=4ax \) is \( 4a \) (chord through focus perpendicular to axis).
name: Latus Rectum of Parabola

text: The tangent to parabola \( y^2=4ax \) at point \((at^2, 2at)\) is:
explanation: Tangent at parametric point \((at^2,2at)\) on \(y^2=4ax\): \( ty = x + at^2 \)
name: Parametric Tangent to Parabola

text: The equation \( \frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \). Here \( a=4, b=3 \), so \( c = \)
name: Foci of Ellipse

text: The sum of focal distances from any point on ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point \(P\) on ellipse: \( PF_1+PF_2 = 2a \) (constant). This defines the ellipse.
name: Focal Distance Sum

text: The directrix of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2=4ax \): directrix is the vertical line \( x = -a \).
name: Directrix of Parabola

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse

text: The difference of focal distances from any point on hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point on hyperbola: \( |PF_1 - PF_2| = 2a \) (constant). This defines the hyperbola.
name: Focal Distance Difference

explanation: Rectangular hyperbola: \( a=b \Rightarrow c=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow e = c/a = \sqrt{2} \).
name: Rectangular Hyperbola
